documentation.value: Defines a virtual server configuration block that handles requests for a specific domain or IP.
The address and port to listen on (e.g., 80, 443 ssl, [::]:80).
The domain names this server block responds to.
Sets configuration for a specific request URI. Supports exact match (=), prefix, and regex (~, ~*) matching.
The URI pattern to match. Prefixes: = (exact), ~ (case-sensitive regex), ~* (case-insensitive regex), ^~ (prefix priority).
Sets the protocol, address, and URI of a proxied server. Used to forward requests to backend servers.
The upstream URL (e.g., http://backend:8080, http://unix:/tmp/sock.sock).
Sets the address and port on which the server will accept requests.
The IP address and/or port to listen on (e.g., 80, 127.0.0.1:8080, [::]:443).
Optional. Enables SSL/TLS for this listening socket.
Optional. Makes this the default server for the address:port pair.
Sets the root directory for serving files. The full file path is constructed by appending the URI to the root.
The filesystem path to serve files from (e.g., /var/www/html).
Defines files that will be used as an index when a directory is requested.
The primary index file (e.g., index.html).
Optional fallback index file (e.g., index.htm, index.php).
Specifies the path to the PEM-format SSL/TLS certificate file for the server.
Path to the certificate file (e.g., /etc/ssl/certs/server.crt).
Defines a group of backend servers for load balancing.
The name used to reference this upstream group in proxy_pass.
Backend server address with optional parameters (weight, max_fails, fail_timeout).
Rewrites the request URI using a regular expression. If the replacement starts with http://, the client gets a redirect.
The regular expression to match against the request URI.
The replacement string. Can use captured groups ($1, $2, etc.).
Optional flag: last (stop and re-search), break (stop), redirect (302), permanent (301).
Adds a custom header to the response. Headers are only added when the response code is 2xx or 3xx unless 'always' is specified.
The header name (e.g., X-Frame-Options, Cache-Control).
The header value.
Optional. If present, the header is added regardless of response code.
Checks for the existence of files in order and uses the first found. The last parameter is a fallback URI or =code.
The first file path to check (e.g., $uri).
Additional file paths to try (e.g., $uri/).
The fallback URI or error code (e.g., /index.html, =404).
